title: MetroCity Composable Character System
description: Build a Node/canvas script that composites MetroCity base body + hair + outfit layers into per-agent spritesheets. Map MetroCity's 24-column layout to the existing 7-frame x 3-direction format. Pre-compose 13 unique agent combinations.

scope.in: Build-time compositing script, MetroCity asset integration, agent appearance mapping, character PNG output
scope.out: Runtime compositing, renderer changes, 32x32 native format support

dod.criterion: 13 unique character PNGs at 112x96 matching current format
Each agent has visually distinct body/hair/outfit combo
Game renders all agents without console errors
LoadedCharacterData interface unchanged

tasks.title: Copy MetroCity assets to repo and scaffold compositing script
tasks.user_scenario: MetroCity source PNGs are available in the repo under public/assets/metrocity/ so the build script can reference them without depending on ~/Downloads.
tasks.scope: Copy MetroCity assets from ~/Downloads/MetroCity/ to public/assets/metrocity/. Create scripts/compose-metrocity.ts with basic scaffold: read source PNGs with pngjs, define 13 agent combination table, write stub output loop creating 112x96 PNGs as char_0..12.png.

dod.criterion: public/assets/metrocity/ contains Character Model.png (768x192), Shadow.png (32x32), Hairs.png (768x256), and Outfit1-6.png (768x32 each)
scripts/compose-metrocity.ts exists and runs via `npx tsx scripts/compose-metrocity.ts` without errors
Script defines AGENT_COMBOS array with 13 entries, each specifying bodyRow, hairRow, outfitIndex, all unique
Script outputs 13 placeholder PNGs (char_0..12.png) to public/assets/characters/ at exactly 112x96

tasks.title: Extract MetroCity frames and map 24-column layout to 7-frame x 3-direction format
tasks.user_scenario: The compositing script correctly extracts individual 32x32 frames from MetroCity's 24-column spritesheet and maps them to the target 7-frame x 3-direction layout.
tasks.scope: Implement core frame extraction logic. MetroCity uses 24 columns = 4 directions x 6 frames. Map walk frames (3 from walk cycle), type/read frames (reuse idle frames). Downscale 32x32 → 16x32 via 2:1 horizontal pixel averaging.

dod.criterion: Script extracts frames from 24-column layout with correct direction offsets (down=0-5, left=6-11, right=12-17, up=18-23)
Output PNGs contain 7 frames per row (walk1-3, type1-2, read1-2) and 3 direction rows (down, up, right)
Each frame downscaled from 32x32 to 16x32 via horizontal pixel averaging, output at exactly 112x96
Base body character is visually recognizable when loaded in the game

tasks.title: Composite body + hair + outfit layers and generate 13 unique agent PNGs
tasks.user_scenario: Each of the 13 agents has a visually distinct character with unique body, hair, and outfit combination, visible in the game.
tasks.scope: Implement multi-layer compositing: body + hair + outfit per agent. Finalize AGENT_COMBOS table. Output char_0..12.png. Update asset-loader loop to load 13 characters. Update PALETTE_COUNT to 13. Add 13th CHARACTER_PALETTES entry.

dod.criterion: Running compose-metrocity.ts produces 13 PNGs at 112x96 with visible body+hair+outfit layers
All 13 agent combos use different body/hair/outfit combinations
asset-loader.ts loads 13 characters
PALETTE_COUNT=13 and CHARACTER_PALETTES has 13 entries
Game renders agents without console errors and characters are visually distinct

tasks.title: Wire agent-registry palette indices to MetroCity characters and verify end-to-end
tasks.user_scenario: Each named agent appears with their intended MetroCity character — correct body/hair/outfit maps to the right person.
tasks.scope: Update agent-registry.json palette indices to match AGENT_COMBOS order. Remove hueShift. Verify pickDiversePalette, matrix effect, and type-check.

dod.criterion: agent-registry.json palette values match AGENT_COMBOS indices
All agent hueShift values are 0
pickDiversePalette handles PALETTE_COUNT=13 without index errors
npm run type-check passes with zero errors
LoadedCharacterData interface unchanged
